S3. Internal consistency

Distribution des items

vars <- paste0("stigma_percept_", 1:8)

d <- descr(
  data_efa[vars],
  stats = c("skewness", "kurtosis"),
  transpose = TRUE
)

# Converts the summarytools object into a usable data.frame
tab <- as.data.frame(d)

# The columns are generally called “Skewness” / “Kurtosis” (spelling varies depending on version).

skew_kurt_table <- data.frame(
  variable = rownames(tab),
  skewness = as.numeric(tab[[grep("skew", names(tab), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)[1]]]),
  kurtosis = as.numeric(tab[[grep("kurt", names(tab), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)[1]]]),
  row.names = NULL
)

skew_kurt_table$skewness <- round(skew_kurt_table$skewness, 2)
skew_kurt_table$kurtosis <- round(skew_kurt_table$kurtosis, 2)


# -----------------------------
# 2)  Global data visualization with dfSummary (mini histograms + NA, etc.)
# -----------------------------
dfs <- dfSummary(
  data_efa[vars],
  style      = "grid",
  graph.col  = TRUE,   # mini-charts in the Graph column
  valid.col  = TRUE,
  na.col     = TRUE
)


# or console/HTML rendering
print(dfs, method = "render")

ICC

data_efa$'item 1'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_1
data_efa$'item 2'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_2
data_efa$'item 3'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_3
data_efa$'item 4'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_4
data_efa$'item 5'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_5
data_efa$'item 6'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_6
data_efa$'item 7'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_7
data_efa$'item 8'<- data_efa$stigma_percept_8

items <- data_efa[, c('item 1',
                      'item 2',
                      'item 3',
                      'item 4',
                      'item 5',
                      'item 6',
                      'item 7',
                      'item 8')]

# Calculate Spearman's correlation matrix
iic <- cor(items, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
round(iic, 2)  %>%
  kbl() %>%
  kable_styling()%>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
item 1 item 2 item 3 item 4 item 5 item 6 item 7 item 8
item 1 1.00 0.45 0.30 -0.06 0.70 0.63 -0.05 0.57
item 2 0.45 1.00 0.21 -0.04 0.41 0.36 -0.03 0.34
item 3 0.30 0.21 1.00 0.18 0.26 0.27 0.27 0.23
item 4 -0.06 -0.04 0.18 1.00 -0.08 -0.09 0.17 0.01
item 5 0.70 0.41 0.26 -0.08 1.00 0.65 -0.06 0.58
item 6 0.63 0.36 0.27 -0.09 0.65 1.00 -0.02 0.61
item 7 -0.05 -0.03 0.27 0.17 -0.06 -0.02 1.00 -0.05
item 8 0.57 0.34 0.23 0.01 0.58 0.61 -0.05 1.00 
# simple Heatmap 
corrplot(iic, method = "color", type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black", # ajoute les valeurs
         tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45,
         diag = FALSE)

ITC

items <- data_efa[vars]

# Cronbach alpha
alpha_result <- psych::alpha(items, check.keys = FALSE)
## Some items ( stigma_percept_4 stigma_percept_7 ) were negatively correlated with the first principal component and 
## probably should be reversed.  
## To do this, run the function again with the 'check.keys=TRUE' option
# Corrected item-total correlations (r.drop)
r_drop <- alpha_result$item.stats$r.drop
names(r_drop) <- colnames(items)
r_drop
## stigma_percept_1 stigma_percept_2 stigma_percept_3 stigma_percept_4 
##      0.637351555      0.424277776      0.420589027     -0.004486333 
## stigma_percept_5 stigma_percept_6 stigma_percept_7 stigma_percept_8 
##      0.613900048      0.580479447      0.053780326      0.563224351
alpha_table <- data.frame(
  item = rownames(alpha_result$item.stats),
  mean = alpha_result$item.stats$mean,
  sd   = alpha_result$item.stats$sd,
  r_drop = alpha_result$item.stats$r.drop,
  alpha_if_deleted = alpha_result$alpha.drop$raw_alpha,
  row.names = NULL
)

alpha_table%>%
  kbl() %>%
  kable_styling()%>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
item mean sd r_drop alpha_if_deleted
stigma_percept_1 6.533067 2.312188 0.6373516 0.6105530
stigma_percept_2 6.753421 2.649128 0.4242778 0.6537762
stigma_percept_3 5.468928 2.477709 0.4205890 0.6553945
stigma_percept_4 5.129703 3.133472 -0.0044863 0.7621395
stigma_percept_5 6.089795 2.343153 0.6139000 0.6148573
stigma_percept_6 6.379418 2.699892 0.5804794 0.6147227
stigma_percept_7 3.814424 2.631443 0.0537803 0.7338558
stigma_percept_8 6.875428 2.558166 0.5632244 0.6216367

Explanations: alpha_result\(total\)raw_alpha: overall alpha

r.drop: corrected item–total correlation (often we like to see > 0.30)

alpha_if_deleted: if deleting the item significantly increases alpha → item possibly problematic

Cronbach

items <- data_efa[vars]

# Cronbach alpha
alpha_result <- psych::alpha(items, check.keys = FALSE)
## Some items ( stigma_percept_4 stigma_percept_7 ) were negatively correlated with the first principal component and 
## probably should be reversed.  
## To do this, run the function again with the 'check.keys=TRUE' option
# Alpha values
alpha_result$total$raw_alpha
## [1] 0.6927691

Omega

# total Omega  (unidimensional)
omega_result <- omega(items, nfactors = 1, fm = "ml")  # fm = "ml"  maximum likelihood

# print results
omega_result
## Omega 
## Call: omegah(m = m, nfactors = nfactors, fm = fm, key = key, flip = flip, 
##     digits = digits, title = title, sl = sl, labels = labels, 
##     plot = plot, n.obs = n.obs, rotate = rotate, Phi = Phi, option = option, 
##     covar = covar)
## Alpha:                 0.73 
## G.6:                   0.77 
## Omega Hierarchical:    0.79 
## Omega H asymptotic:    1.03 
## Omega Total            0.77 
## 
## Schmid Leiman Factor loadings greater than  0.2 
##                      g  F1*   h2   h2   u2 p2 com
## stigma_percept_1  0.83      0.69 0.69 0.31  1   1
## stigma_percept_2  0.54      0.29 0.29 0.71  1   1
## stigma_percept_3  0.33           0.11 0.89  1   1
## stigma_percept_4-                0.01 0.99  1   1
## stigma_percept_5  0.84      0.71 0.71 0.29  1   1
## stigma_percept_6  0.79      0.62 0.62 0.38  1   1
## stigma_percept_7-                0.00 1.00  1   1
## stigma_percept_8  0.71      0.50 0.50 0.50  1   1
## 
## With Sums of squares  of:
##   g F1*  h2 
## 2.9 0.0 1.7 
## 
## general/max  1.71   max/min =   2.064481e+16
## mean percent general =  1    with sd =  0 and cv of  0 
## Explained Common Variance of the general factor =  1 
## 
## The degrees of freedom are 20  and the fit is  0.22 
## The number of observations was  3508  with Chi Square =  777.29  with prob <  9.3e-152
## The root mean square of the residuals is  0.08 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.09
## RMSEA index =  0.104  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.098 0.11
## BIC =  614.03
## 
## Compare this with the adequacy of just a general factor and no group factors
## The degrees of freedom for just the general factor are 20  and the fit is  0.22 
## The number of observations was  3508  with Chi Square =  777.29  with prob <  9.3e-152
## The root mean square of the residuals is  0.08 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.09 
## 
## RMSEA index =  0.104  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.098 0.11
## BIC =  614.03 
## 
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                  g F1*
## Correlation of scores with factors            0.94   0
## Multiple R square of scores with factors      0.89   0
## Minimum correlation of factor score estimates 0.78  -1
## 
##  Total, General and Subset omega for each subset
##                                                  g  F1*
## Omega total for total scores and subscales    0.77 0.79
## Omega general for total scores and subscales  0.79 0.79
## Omega group for total scores and subscales    0.00 0.00

Floor ceiling

# items = data.frame of numeric items
floor_ceiling <- t(sapply(items, function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n == 0) return(c(Floor = NA_real_, Ceiling = NA_real_))

  floor_effect   <- sum(x == min(x)) / n * 100
  ceiling_effect <- sum(x == max(x)) / n * 100

  c(Floor = round(floor_effect, 1), Ceiling = round(ceiling_effect, 1))
}))

floor_ceiling <- as.data.frame(floor_ceiling)
floor_ceiling$item <- rownames(floor_ceiling)
rownames(floor_ceiling) <- NULL



stats <- data.frame(
  item = names(items),
  n    = sapply(items, function(x) sum(is.finite(x))),
  min  = sapply(items, function(x) min(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  max  = sapply(items, function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  mean = sapply(items, function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  sd   = sapply(items, function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
)

report_tbl <- stats %>%
  left_join(floor_ceiling, by = "item") %>%
  mutate(
    mean = round(mean, 2),
    sd   = round(sd, 2)
  )

report_tbl %>%
  kbl(caption = "Descriptifs + Floor/Ceiling effects by item") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
Descriptifs + Floor/Ceiling effects by item
item n min max mean sd Floor Ceiling
stigma_percept_1 3508 0 10 6.53 2.31 0.7 10.0
stigma_percept_2 3508 0 10 6.75 2.65 1.4 21.5
stigma_percept_3 3508 0 10 5.47 2.48 1.1 6.7
stigma_percept_4 3508 0 10 5.13 3.13 5.7 16.8
stigma_percept_5 3508 0 10 6.09 2.34 1.2 7.0
stigma_percept_6 3508 0 10 6.38 2.70 2.5 14.4
stigma_percept_7 3508 0 10 3.81 2.63 10.2 2.2
stigma_percept_8 3508 0 10 6.88 2.56 2.0 16.3

S4. Structural validity

unidimensional CFA

data_efa <- data_efa %>%
  rename(
    item1 = `item 1`,
    item2 = `item 2`,
    item3 = `item 3`,
    item4 = `item 4`,
    item5 = `item 5`,
    item6 = `item 6`,
    item7 = `item 7`,
    item8 = `item 8`
  )

cfa_model <- '
BIPQ =~ item1 + item2 + item3 + item4 +
        item5 + item6 + item7 + item8
'

fit <- cfa(cfa_model, data = data_efa, estimator = "MLR") # MLR = robust to non-normality
summary(fit, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-20 ended normally after 33 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        16
## 
##   Number of observations                          3508
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                               Standard      Scaled
##   Test Statistic                               778.436     635.393
##   Degrees of freedom                                20          20
##   P-value (Chi-square)                           0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  1.225
##     Yuan-Bentler correction (Mplus variant)                       
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                              9101.381    6877.832
##   Degrees of freedom                                28          28
##   P-value                                        0.000       0.000
##   Scaling correction factor                                  1.323
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    0.916       0.910
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       0.883       0.874
##                                                                   
##   Robust Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                         0.917
##   Robust Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                            0.884
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)             -62363.541  -62363.541
##   Scaling correction factor                                  1.146
##       for the MLR correction                                      
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)     -61974.323  -61974.323
##   Scaling correction factor                                  1.190
##       for the MLR correction                                      
##                                                                   
##   Akaike (AIC)                              124759.081  124759.081
##   Bayesian (BIC)                            124857.686  124857.686
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)     124806.847  124806.847
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.104       0.094
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.098       0.088
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.110       0.099
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                    0.000       0.000
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                    1.000       1.000
##                                                                   
##   Robust RMSEA                                               0.104
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower                     0.097
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper                     0.111
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA <= 0.050                         0.000
##   P-value H_0: Robust RMSEA >= 0.080                         1.000
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.069       0.069
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                             Sandwich
##   Information bread                           Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Latent Variables:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   BIPQ =~                                                               
##     item1             1.000                               1.926    0.833
##     item2             0.745    0.024   31.356    0.000    1.434    0.541
##     item3             0.422    0.027   15.884    0.000    0.812    0.328
##     item4            -0.157    0.033   -4.712    0.000   -0.302   -0.096
##     item5             1.027    0.018   58.211    0.000    1.977    0.844
##     item6             1.101    0.023   48.611    0.000    2.120    0.785
##     item7            -0.079    0.029   -2.704    0.007   -0.151   -0.058
##     item8             0.942    0.023   41.789    0.000    1.814    0.709
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .item1             1.637    0.069   23.756    0.000    1.637    0.306
##    .item2             4.961    0.125   39.767    0.000    4.961    0.707
##    .item3             5.477    0.120   45.803    0.000    5.477    0.892
##    .item4             9.724    0.156   62.216    0.000    9.724    0.991
##    .item5             1.580    0.074   21.461    0.000    1.580    0.288
##    .item6             2.794    0.115   24.207    0.000    2.794    0.383
##    .item7             6.900    0.134   51.481    0.000    6.900    0.997
##    .item8             3.253    0.121   26.812    0.000    3.253    0.497
##     BIPQ              3.708    0.128   29.078    0.000    1.000    1.000
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate
##     item1             0.694
##     item2             0.293
##     item3             0.108
##     item4             0.009
##     item5             0.712
##     item6             0.617
##     item7             0.003
##     item8             0.503
fitMeasures(fit, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.916 0.883 0.104 0.069
semPaths(fit, what = "path", whatLabels = "std", layout = "tree",
         color = "white", edge.color = "black", fade = FALSE,
         nCharNodes = 0, edge.label.cex = 0.9,
         sizeMan = 8, sizeLat = 10, mar = c(5, 5, 5, 5))

CFA comparison

For model comparison, CFA does not work, probably due to a problem related to covariance.

### model 1 

cfa_model_multi <- '
F1 =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8
F2 =~ stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7
# Item 2 n’appartient à aucun facteur, il reste indépendant
'
fit_multi <- cfa(cfa_model_multi, data = data_efa, estimator = "MLR")
summary(fit_multi, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-20 did NOT end normally after 486 iterations
## ** WARNING ** Estimates below are most likely unreliable
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        15
## 
##   Number of observations                          3508
## 
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                             Sandwich
##   Information bread                           Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Latent Variables:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all 
##   F1 =~                                                                      
##     stigma_prcpt_1      1.000                                 1.903     0.823
##     stigma_prcpt_5      1.039       NA                        1.977     0.844
##     stigma_prcpt_6      1.124       NA                        2.139     0.792
##     stigma_prcpt_8      0.962       NA                        1.830     0.716
##   F2 =~                                                                      
##     stigma_prcpt_3      1.000                               173.820    69.896
##     stigma_prcpt_4      0.000       NA                        0.007     0.002
##     stigma_prcpt_7      0.000       NA                        0.010     0.004
## 
## Covariances:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all 
##   F1 ~~                                                                      
##     F2                  1.706       NA                        0.005     0.005
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all 
##    .stigma_prcpt_1      1.724       NA                        1.724     0.323
##    .stigma_prcpt_5      1.580       NA                        1.580     0.288
##    .stigma_prcpt_6      2.712       NA                        2.712     0.372
##    .stigma_prcpt_8      3.193       NA                        3.193     0.488
##    .stigma_prcpt_3 -30207.354       NA                   -30207.354 -4884.464
##    .stigma_prcpt_4      9.816       NA                        9.816     1.000
##    .stigma_prcpt_7      6.922       NA                        6.922     1.000
##     F1                  3.621       NA                        1.000     1.000
##     F2              30213.538       NA                        1.000     1.000
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate  
##     stigma_prcpt_1      0.677
##     stigma_prcpt_5      0.712
##     stigma_prcpt_6      0.628
##     stigma_prcpt_8      0.512
##     stigma_prcpt_3         NA
##     stigma_prcpt_4      0.000
##     stigma_prcpt_7      0.000
### model 2 

cfa_model_multi_2 <- '
F1 =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_2 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8
F2 =~ stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7
'
fit_multi_2 <- cfa(cfa_model_multi_2, data = data_efa, estimator = "MLR")

summary(fit_multi_2, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-20 did NOT end normally after 689 iterations
## ** WARNING ** Estimates below are most likely unreliable
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        17
## 
##   Number of observations                          3508
## 
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                             Sandwich
##   Information bread                           Observed
##   Observed information based on                Hessian
## 
## Latent Variables:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all  
##   F1 =~                                                                       
##     stigma_prcpt_1      1.000                                 1.926      0.833
##     stigma_prcpt_2      0.745       NA                        1.435      0.542
##     stigma_prcpt_5      1.025       NA                        1.975      0.843
##     stigma_prcpt_6      1.100       NA                        2.118      0.785
##     stigma_prcpt_8      0.941       NA                        1.813      0.709
##   F2 =~                                                                       
##     stigma_prcpt_3      1.000                               307.573    123.646
##     stigma_prcpt_4      0.000       NA                        0.004      0.001
##     stigma_prcpt_7      0.000       NA                        0.006      0.002
## 
## Covariances:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all  
##   F1 ~~                                                                       
##     F2                  1.755       NA                        0.003      0.003
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate    Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv    Std.all  
##    .stigma_prcpt_1      1.633       NA                        1.633      0.306
##    .stigma_prcpt_2      4.955       NA                        4.955      0.706
##    .stigma_prcpt_5      1.589       NA                        1.589      0.289
##    .stigma_prcpt_6      2.799       NA                        2.799      0.384
##    .stigma_prcpt_8      3.254       NA                        3.254      0.497
##    .stigma_prcpt_3 -94594.835       NA                   -94594.835 -15287.259
##    .stigma_prcpt_4      9.816       NA                        9.816      1.000
##    .stigma_prcpt_7      6.922       NA                        6.922      1.000
##     F1                  3.711       NA                        1.000      1.000
##     F2              94601.023       NA                        1.000      1.000
## 
## R-Square:
##                    Estimate  
##     stigma_prcpt_1      0.694
##     stigma_prcpt_2      0.294
##     stigma_prcpt_5      0.711
##     stigma_prcpt_6      0.616
##     stigma_prcpt_8      0.503
##     stigma_prcpt_3         NA
##     stigma_prcpt_4      0.000
##     stigma_prcpt_7      0.000

S5. Hypothesis testing

Convergent validity

Simple correlation

dat_bis <- dat %>%
  dplyr::select(score_PHQ9, Score_autostigma, Score_BIPQ, Score_whodas, score_GAD7)

cor_spearman2 <- cor(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$score_PHQ9, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
cor.test(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$score_PHQ9, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  dat_bis$Score_BIPQ and dat_bis$score_PHQ9
## S = 3123072143, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5648197
cor_spearman3 <- cor(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$score_GAD7, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")

cor.test(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$score_GAD7, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  dat_bis$Score_BIPQ and dat_bis$score_GAD7
## S = 578381722, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.3731228

Latent model

BIPQ ~ PHQ9

simple model (free correlation)

var_phq <- c("id_unik", "score_PHQ9", "interet_plaisir",    "triste_deprime",   "sommeil",  "fatigue",  "appetit",  "opinion_soi",  "concentration",    "activite", "consequence_pb",
             
             "Score_BIPQ", "stigma_percept_1", "stigma_percept_2", "stigma_percept_3", "stigma_percept_4","stigma_percept_5", "stigma_percept_6", "stigma_percept_7", "stigma_percept_8"
)
data_phq <-dat[, c(var_phq)]


cfa_model_PHQ <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
PHQ9 =~ interet_plaisir + triste_deprime + sommeil + fatigue + appetit + opinion_soi + concentration + activite + consequence_pb
'
fit_PHQ <- cfa(cfa_model_PHQ, data = data_phq, estimator = "MLR")
#summary(fit_PHQ, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
fitMeasures(fit_PHQ, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.894 0.878 0.059 0.051

with correlation ==1

cfa_PHQ_corr <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + 
        stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + 
        stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2

PHQ9 =~ interet_plaisir + triste_deprime + sommeil + fatigue + appetit + opinion_soi + concentration + activite + consequence_pb

# correlation constrained to 1
BIPQ ~~ 1*PHQ9
'

fit_PHQ_corr <- cfa(
  cfa_PHQ_corr,
  data = data_phq,
  estimator = "MLR"
)

#summary(fit_PHQ_corr, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)
fitMeasures(fit_PHQ_corr, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.885 0.869 0.061 0.079
# test

lavTestLRT(fit_PHQ, fit_PHQ_corr)
## 
## Scaled Chi-Squared Difference Test (method = "satorra.bentler.2001")
## 
## lavaan->lavTestLRT():  
##    lavaan NOTE: The "Chisq" column contains standard test statistics, not the 
##    robust test that should be reported per model. A robust difference test is 
##    a function of two standard (not robust) statistics.
## 
##               Df    AIC    BIC  Chisq Chisq diff   RMSEA Df diff Pr(>Chisq)    
## fit_PHQ      118 210345 210561 1535.4                                          
## fit_PHQ_corr 119 210459 210668 1651.0     100.14 0.18093       1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Explanations: If the test is significant: the constraint (correlation = 1) degrades the model, so the factors are not equivalent.

1 factor model

cfa_model_PHQ_1 <- '
BIPQPHQ9 =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
 + interet_plaisir + triste_deprime + sommeil + fatigue + appetit + opinion_soi + concentration + activite + consequence_pb
'
fit_PHQ_1 <- cfa(cfa_model_PHQ_1, data = data_phq, estimator = "MLR")
#summary(fit_PHQ_1, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)

fitMeasures(fit_PHQ_1, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.810 0.783 0.078 0.068

BIPQ~GAD7

Simple model (free correlation)

var_gad <- c("id_unik", "score_GAD7", "nerveux",    "inquiet",  "inquiet_exces",    "difficult_detente",    "agitation",    "tendance_contrarie",   "sentiment_peur",
             "Score_BIPQ", "stigma_percept_1", "stigma_percept_2", "stigma_percept_3", "stigma_percept_4","stigma_percept_5", "stigma_percept_6", "stigma_percept_7", "stigma_percept_8"
)
data_gad<-dat[, c(var_gad)]

cfa_model_GAD <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
GAD =~ nerveux + inquiet + inquiet_exces + difficult_detente + agitation + tendance_contrarie + sentiment_peur
'

fit_GAD_Missing <- cfa(
  cfa_model_GAD,
  data = data_gad,
  estimator = "MLR",
  missing = "fiml"  #management of missing values, as there are many for the GAD-7
)

#summary(fit_GAD_Missing, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)

fitMeasures(fit_GAD_Missing, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.926 0.912 0.054 0.048

with correlation = 1

cfa_GAD_corr <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + 
        stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + 
        stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2

GAD =~ nerveux + inquiet + inquiet_exces + difficult_detente + 
       agitation + tendance_contrarie + sentiment_peur

# correlation constrained to 1
BIPQ ~~ 1*GAD
'

fit_GAD_corr <- cfa(
  cfa_GAD_corr,
  data = data_gad,
  estimator = "MLR",
  missing = "fiml" 
)
## Warning: lavaan->lav_start_check_cov():  
##    starting values imply a correlation larger than 1; variables involved are: 
##    BIPQ GAD
#summary(fit_GAD_corr, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)
fitMeasures(fit_GAD_corr, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.906 0.890 0.060 0.139
# test

lavTestLRT(fit_GAD_Missing, fit_GAD_corr)
## 
## Scaled Chi-Squared Difference Test (method = "satorra.bentler.2001")
## 
## lavaan->lavTestLRT():  
##    lavaan NOTE: The "Chisq" column contains standard test statistics, not the 
##    robust test that should be reported per model. A robust difference test is 
##    a function of two standard (not robust) statistics.
## 
##                 Df    AIC    BIC   Chisq Chisq diff Df diff Pr(>Chisq)    
## fit_GAD_Missing 89 157182 157466  995.07                                  
## fit_GAD_corr    90 157424 157701 1238.80     175.92       1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

1 factor model

cfa_model_GAD_1 <- '
BIPQGAD =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
 + nerveux + inquiet + inquiet_exces + difficult_detente + agitation + tendance_contrarie + sentiment_peur
'


fit_GAD_Missing_1 <- cfa(
  cfa_model_GAD_1,
  data = data_gad,
  estimator = "MLR",
  missing = "fiml"
)
#summary(fit_GAD_Missing_1, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)


fitMeasures(fit_GAD_Missing_1, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.734 0.689 0.101 0.124

Discriminant validity

Simple correlations

cor_spearman <- cor(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$Score_autostigma, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
cor.test(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$Score_autostigma, method = "spearman")
## Warning in cor.test.default(dat_bis$Score_BIPQ, dat_bis$Score_autostigma, :
## Impossible de calculer la p-value exacte avec des ex-aequos
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  dat_bis$Score_BIPQ and dat_bis$Score_autostigma
## S = 3617856781, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4889072

Latent model

Simple model (free correlation)

var_sss <- c("id_unik", "Score_autostigma", "stigma_auto_1",    "stigma_auto_2",    "stigma_auto_3",    "stigma_auto_4",    "stigma_auto_5",    "stigma_auto_6",    "stigma_auto_7", "stigma_auto_8", "stigma_auto_9",
             "Score_BIPQ", "stigma_percept_1", "stigma_percept_2", "stigma_percept_3", "stigma_percept_4","stigma_percept_5", "stigma_percept_6", "stigma_percept_7", "stigma_percept_8"
)
data_sss <-dat[, c(var_sss)]

cfa_model_SSS <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
SSS =~ stigma_auto_1 + stigma_auto_2 + stigma_auto_3 + stigma_auto_4 + stigma_auto_5 + stigma_auto_6 + stigma_auto_7 + stigma_auto_8 + stigma_auto_9
'
fit_SSS <- cfa(cfa_model_SSS, data = data_sss, estimator = "MLR")
#summary(fit_SSS, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
fitMeasures(fit_SSS, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.854 0.832 0.094 0.076

with correlation = 1

cfa_SSS_corr <- '
BIPQ =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + 
        stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + 
        stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2

SSS =~ stigma_auto_1 + stigma_auto_2 + stigma_auto_3 + stigma_auto_4 + stigma_auto_5 + stigma_auto_6 + stigma_auto_7 + stigma_auto_8 + stigma_auto_9

# correlation constrained to 1
BIPQ ~~ 1*SSS
'

fit_SSS_corr <- cfa(
  cfa_SSS_corr,
  data = data_sss,
  estimator = "MLR"
)
## Warning: lavaan->lav_start_check_cov():  
##    starting values imply a correlation larger than 1; variables involved are: 
##    BIPQ SSS
#summary(fit_SSS_corr, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)
fitMeasures(fit_SSS_corr, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.850 0.828 0.095 0.114
# test 

lavTestLRT(fit_SSS, fit_SSS_corr)
## 
## Scaled Chi-Squared Difference Test (method = "satorra.bentler.2001")
## 
## lavaan->lavTestLRT():  
##    lavaan NOTE: The "Chisq" column contains standard test statistics, not the 
##    robust test that should be reported per model. A robust difference test is 
##    a function of two standard (not robust) statistics.
## 
##               Df    AIC    BIC  Chisq Chisq diff   RMSEA Df diff Pr(>Chisq)    
## fit_SSS      118 210626 210842 3760.4                                          
## fit_SSS_corr 119 210742 210951 3878.1     108.46 0.18281       1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

1 factor model

cfa_model_SSS_1 <- '
BIPQSSS =~ stigma_percept_1 + stigma_percept_5 + stigma_percept_6 + stigma_percept_8 + stigma_percept_3 + stigma_percept_4 + stigma_percept_7 + stigma_percept_2
 + stigma_auto_1 + stigma_auto_2 + stigma_auto_3 + stigma_auto_4 + stigma_auto_5 + stigma_auto_6 + stigma_auto_7 + stigma_auto_8 + stigma_auto_9
'
fit_SSS_1 <- cfa(cfa_model_SSS_1, data = data_sss, estimator = "MLR")
#summary(fit_SSS_1, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)

fitMeasures(fit_SSS_1, c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea", "srmr"))
##   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr 
## 0.645 0.594 0.146 0.110

S6. Incremental validity

With anova

dat_bis <- dat %>%
  dplyr::select(score_PHQ9, Score_autostigma, Score_BIPQ, Score_whodas, score_GAD7, age, Sexe)

dat_clean <- dat_bis[complete.cases(dat_bis[, c(
  "age",
  "Sexe",
  "Score_whodas",
  "score_PHQ9",
  "Score_autostigma",
  "score_GAD7",
  "Score_BIPQ"
)]), ]

# Model A : WHODAS ~ Age + Sex
modelA <- lm(Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe, data = dat_clean)

# Model B : WHODAS ~ Age + Sex + PHQ9 + SSS + GAD
modelB <- lm(Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7, data = dat_clean)

# Model C : WHODAS ~ Age + Sex + PHQ9 + SSS + GAD + BIPQ
modelC <- lm(Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ, data = dat_clean)

anova_result <- anova(modelA, modelB, modelC)
anova_result
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Model 1: Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe
## Model 2: Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7
## Model 3: Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + 
##     Score_BIPQ
##   Res.Df    RSS Df Sum of Sq        F Pr(>F)    
## 1   1582 135609                                 
## 2   1579 102237  3     33373 171.9391 <2e-16 ***
## 3   1578 102094  1       143   2.2032 0.1379    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
r2 <- function(m) summary(m)$r.squared

model1 <- lm(
  Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe,
  data = dat_clean
)

model2 <- lm(
  Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7,
  data = dat_clean
)

model3 <- lm(
  Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
  data = dat_clean
)

r2_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Step 1: age + sex",
            "Step 2: + PHQ-9 + SSS-S + GAD-7",
            "Step 3: + BIPQ"),
  R2 = c(r2(model1), r2(model2), r2(model3))
)

r2_table$Delta_R2 <- c(NA, diff(r2_table$R2))

r2_table$R2_percent <- round(r2_table$R2 * 100, 2)
r2_table$Delta_R2_percent <- round(r2_table$Delta_R2 * 100, 2)

r2_table %>% kbl() %>%
  kable_styling()%>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
Model R2 Delta_R2 R2_percent Delta_R2_percent
Step 1: age + sex 0.0520944 NA 5.21 NA
Step 2: + PHQ-9 + SSS-S + GAD-7 0.2853679 0.2332735 28.54 23.33
Step 3: + BIPQ 0.2863643 0.0009964 28.64 0.10 

r2 <- function(m) summary(m)$r.squared

model_full <- lm(
  Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
  data = dat_clean
)

r2_full <- r2(model_full)

unique_table_block <- tibble(
  Predictor = c(
    "Age + sex",
    "PHQ-9",
    "Self-stigma",
    "GAD-7",
    "BIPQ"
  ),
  Unique_R2 = c(
    r2_full - r2(lm(
      Score_whodas ~ score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
      data = dat_clean
    )),
    
    r2_full - r2(lm(
      Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
      data = dat_clean
    )),
    
    r2_full - r2(lm(
      Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
      data = dat_clean
    )),
    
    r2_full - r2(lm(
      Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + Score_BIPQ,
      data = dat_clean
    )),
    
    r2_full - r2(lm(
      Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7,
      data = dat_clean
    ))
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Unique_R2 * 100,
    Label = paste0(round(Percent, 2), "%")
  )

unique_table_block %>% kbl() %>%
  kable_styling()%>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
Predictor Unique_R2 Percent Label
Age + sex 0.0135124 1.3512382 1.35%
PHQ-9 0.0655371 6.5537085 6.55%
Self-stigma 0.0020984 0.2098414 0.21%
GAD-7 0.0231791 2.3179129 2.32%
BIPQ 0.0009964 0.0996361 0.1%

with yhat

## Commonality analysis 

res_lm <- lm(Score_whodas ~ age + Sexe + score_PHQ9 + Score_autostigma + score_GAD7 + Score_BIPQ,
             data = dat_clean)
res_dom <- calc.yhat(res_lm)
boot.out = boot(data=dat_clean, yhat::boot.yhat, 1000, 
                lmOut=res_lm, regrout0=res_dom)

boot_results = booteval.yhat(res_dom, boot.out,bty="perc",.95,3)

results_filtered <- boot_results$combCIaps["age,Sexe,score_PHQ9,Score_autostigma,score_GAD7", "Score_BIPQ.Inc"] # First, the proportion of Whodas variance explained by the three questions, then the incremental validity of the BIPQ.

print(results_filtered)
## [1] "0.001(0.000,0.006)"
